pages.text: Pinna is a genus of mollusk that hails all the way from the Carboniferous into the present day, with extant and extinct forms being found across the globe. Pinna is a large bivalve, getting up to 80 cm in length. Whilst being found as far back as the Carboniferous, it takes until the Jurassic and Cretaceous for Pinna to gain a proper footing. Outside of its long temporal range, Pinna is also well known for its sea silk, most commonly harvested to make gloves,
the palliums of early Christians and Roman cloaks, robes, and other articles of clothing, due to its lightness and warmth.
